notes: Coverage is the last 7 days in Asia/Taipei.

Google-account-backed workbook observation attempted through Codex in-app Browser only; the browser page attach failed, so intern workbook freshness is marked google_browser_blocked and prior intern/talent outputs were kept.
Items are deduplicated against the prior week by id and source URL; this week contains six new radar items.

googleWorkbook.nextAction: Open the workbook in the in-app Browser with egroup.kevin@gmail.com visible, then rerun the intern export/update path.

highlights.title: Google workbook freshness is still blocked at the visible browser layer
highlights.why: The connector freeze was honored again: no Google Drive or Sheets connector/API fallback was used after the in-app Browser could not attach to the Sheets page.
highlights.next: Keep the prior intern and talent-pool data live, and rerun the workbook export only after the signed-in Google page is visible.

highlights.title: Codex moved further into long-running desktop work
highlights.why: OpenAI announced Codex inside the ChatGPT desktop app and broader Work capabilities, which raises the bar for evidence, approvals, and task state in Kevin-style automations.
highlights.next: Keep logging source surface, approval boundary, blocker label, and production proof for every recurring automation.

highlights.title: Community platforms are making trust and policy loops more explicit
highlights.why: Patreon is previewing policy updates with creator feedback, while Discord continues shipping operational patch notes; both are useful patterns for member/community workflow design.
highlights.next: Use a lightweight feedback window before adding any new FamilyFin challenge, badge, or membership mechanic.

items.title: Intern workbook refresh remains blocked by the in-app Browser
items.oneLine: The Google Sheets workbook could not be observed through the required visible browser surface, so the run kept the last verified intern snapshot and did not use frozen Google connectors.
facts: The browser connection succeeded for documentation, but opening the workbook failed with a webview attach timeout.
No Google Drive, Sheets, or other Google connector/API fallback was used.
yourTake: This is a real freshness blocker, not proof that the spreadsheet is unchanged.
The public snapshot and talent-pool pages can stay live because they remain based on the last verified workbook timestamp and local historical files.
actions: Rerun workbook observation after the in-app Browser can show the expected signed-in account and file title.
Do not claim a new source modified_time until the browser or an explicitly approved non-frozen path proves it.

credibility.reason: Direct browser attempt failed and connector fallback was intentionally not used.

followups: Open workbook visibly in Codex in-app Browser and rerun intern export.

items.title: InfoCenter rhythm aggregate stayed stable after refresh
items.oneLine: The read-only work-rhythm helper ran, but the public aggregate file has no new content delta from the last successful snapshot.
facts: Current aggregate summary remains 500 scanned events, 73 people, 480 total signals, 0 high-confidence signals, and 480 medium-confidence signals.
The output remains aggregate-only and excludes raw titles, comment bodies, salary amounts, bank data, IDs, and full addresses.
No InfoCenter write action was performed.
yourTake: Stable rhythm data is still useful for assignment timing and check-ins, but it is not payroll or reimbursement evidence.
The 73-person count should remain a tracking item until a future live delta explains the increase from earlier 72-person runs.
actions: Use rhythm data only as planning evidence.
Keep the people-count tracking item open for the next run.

credibility.reason: Helper completed, but file content did not change from the prior aggregate snapshot.

followups: Check whether peopleCount changes after the next readable live run.

items.title: Codex joins the ChatGPT desktop work surface
items.oneLine: OpenAI says Codex is now part of the ChatGPT desktop app, with direct editing, PR review, multi-repo projects, and clearer task progress.
facts: The Codex changelog lists the July 9 update: Codex joins the ChatGPT desktop app on macOS and Windows.
The same changelog calls out Markdown/code editing, inline annotations, GitHub PR review in the sidebar, and work across repositories in one project.
OpenAI also describes ChatGPT Work as a long-running agent that can gather information across apps and workflows and produce finished materials.
yourTake: This reinforces Kevin’s need for durable task state: source surface, changed files, approval gates, and proof should be first-class in automation closeouts.
Desktop app unification may reduce context switching, but it also makes it easier to blur read-only observation and write actions unless run logs stay explicit.
actions: Keep blocker labels and source-access notes in automation JSON.
Check next week whether desktop/browser changes improve visible Google evidence capture.

credibility.reason: Primary OpenAI product and changelog sources.

followups: Review next Codex changelog for browser/account evidence capture improvements.

items.title: Government security case study shows agentic code review at public-sector scale
items.oneLine: Anthropic says Alberta used Claude Code to scan 466 million lines in 20 hours and remediate security gaps, a useful benchmark for audit-first modernization workflows.
facts: Anthropic published the Alberta government case study on July 6, 2026.
The case study says a government team scanned 466 million lines of code in 20 hours and used Claude Code with Opus and Sonnet models for vulnerability review and fixes.
The same post frames technical debt and security vulnerabilities as common public-sector modernization problems.
yourTake: The relevant takeaway for Kevin is not scale envy; it is workflow design: separate scan, remediation, verification, and reusable tooling.
For InfoCenter or FamilyFin internal tools, start with read-only audit packets before allowing write or deploy actions.
actions: Use scan-remediate-verify as a pattern for future internal tool hardening.
Require proof artifacts before promoting any automated fixer into a write-capable workflow.

sources.label: Anthropic - Government of Alberta Claude cybersecurity case study

followups: Extract a small audit-first modernization checklist if this pattern repeats.

items.title: Patreon policy preview is a useful community trust pattern
items.oneLine: Patreon’s mid-year policy update uses creator feedback, advance questions, and an extended Discord Q&A window before finalizing guideline changes.
facts: Patreon updated its mid-year 2026 policy-development post on July 6, 2026.
The post says Patreon shares proposed guideline updates early through its Creator Policy Engagement Program.
The July cycle includes pre-submitted questions and a two-week Q&A window in the Patreon Creator Community Discord server.
yourTake: For FamilyFin or member growth features, trust-building matters more than decorative gamification.
Before launching challenges, badges, or visible status, use a small feedback window so users can object to pressure, privacy, or fairness problems.
actions: Draft one challenge-feedback prompt before any new reward loop.
Keep community mechanics non-monetary and opt-in until there is manual proof.
followups: Create one feedback-first challenge prototype for the next content cycle.

items.title: Taiwan public-source watch points to practical finance and staffing follow-ups
items.oneLine: FSC consumer education pages keep virtual-asset and scam-prevention materials visible, while MOHW job listings show ongoing social-work-related staffing demand.
facts: FSC’s consumer video page lists scam-prevention and virtual-asset education materials, including a virtual-asset service provider awareness video.
MOHW job listings in this window include Social Assistance and Social Work Department research and acting-researcher openings with July deadlines.
These are source-watch signals, not direct policy-change proof.
yourTake: The finance-education path should remain practical: platform verification, scam prevention, and complaint channels before any investment discussion.
The staffing signal is useful for workforce radar but should not be overread without comparing longer-term hiring history.
actions: Use FSC materials as source links for a virtual-asset consumer checklist.
Keep MOHW staffing listings in the tracking backlog for social-work capacity watch.

credibility.reason: Primary official pages, but the item is a source-watch interpretation rather than a single new policy announcement.

followups: Confirm newest FSC virtual-asset explainer before publishing reader-facing advice.
